skill.name: notion-meeting-intelligence
skill.description: Prepare meeting agendas, pre-reads, and follow-up pages using Notion context and Codex research.
skill.summary: Build meeting briefs from Notion context, tailored templates, and cited research.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static command and weak-crypto detections were mostly false positives caused by Markdown backticks and ordinary meeting text. The confirmed risk is the skill's legitimate Notion MCP workflow, which can connect to an external service and create or update workspace pages after user authentication.

medium_findings.title: External Notion MCP Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add and authenticate a remote Notion MCP server, then use Notion tools to search, fetch, create, and update pages. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can expose meeting data and modify workspace content if users grant broad Notion access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly configure a remote Notion MCP URL and use Notion search, fetch, create, and update tools. The workflow is user-authorized and aligned with the skill purpose, so the concern is data-access risk rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Static Command Detections Are Mostly Markdown References
low_findings.description: Many static command alerts point to Markdown backticks around Notion tool names, local reference paths, and example filenames. The real shell commands are limited to Codex MCP setup and login instructions for the Notion integration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown documentation and tool labels, not executable code. No evidence found of hidden scripts, dynamic command construction, or automatic command execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts on the flagged lines do not correspond to cryptographic algorithms or hashing code. The lines contain skill metadata and meeting input instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language descriptions and meeting preparation instructions. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic implementation.

content.value_statement: Meeting preparation often misses prior decisions, open risks, and attendee-specific context. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code build Notion-based agendas and pre-reads from existing workspace knowledge.
actual_capabilities: Collects meeting goals, attendees, timing, decisions needed, and prior materials before drafting.
Uses Notion search and fetch tools to gather related notes, specs, OKRs, and action items.
Selects meeting formats for status updates, decisions, planning, retrospectives, one-on-ones, and brainstorming.
Drafts Notion agendas and pre-reads with source links, owners, timeboxes, risks, and prep asks.
Adds concise Codex research with source links when outside context is useful.
Updates existing Notion pages as meeting details change.
limitations: Requires a connected Notion MCP server and user OAuth authorization.
Can only access Notion pages and databases allowed by the user's integration permissions.
Does not record live meetings, transcribe audio, or verify attendee availability.
External research claims still need source review before they are shared.

use_cases.title: Executive Review Preparation
use_cases.description: Create a concise pre-read with goals, prior decisions, metrics, risks, and decision points for leadership review.

use_cases.title: Project Decision Meeting
use_cases.description: Gather related specs and notes, then prepare an agenda focused on tradeoffs, owners, and approval criteria.

use_cases.title: Recurring Team Planning
use_cases.description: Turn past action items and current priorities into a structured planning agenda with timeboxes and follow-up owners.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Basic Agenda
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to prepare a Notion agenda for a 30-minute project sync. Ask me for the goal, attendees, date, and needed decisions before drafting.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Decision Pre-Read
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to gather Notion context for a decision meeting. Find prior notes, summarize the options, list risks, and draft a pre-read with decision criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Planning Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a planning agenda from Notion project notes, open action items, and current goals. Include timeboxes, owners, and expected outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Executive Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to prepare an executive meeting brief. Combine Notion history, unresolved decisions, stakeholder-specific context, and cited Codex research into a concise page.

output_examples.input: Prepare a decision meeting for choosing the next billing architecture.
output_examples.output: A Notion pre-read with meeting objective, linked prior specs, three options, tradeoffs, risks, decision criteria, agenda timeboxes, and named owners.

output_examples.input: Create a weekly product review agenda from recent Notion notes.
output_examples.output: A structured agenda with current goals, metric review, open blockers, customer signals, discussion owners, follow-up actions, and linked source pages.

output_examples.input: Help me prep a stakeholder briefing for a launch plan.
output_examples.output: A concise briefing page with launch context, attendee-specific questions, dependencies, external research links, decisions needed, and next-step owners.

best_practices: Grant the Notion MCP integration only the workspace pages needed for meeting preparation.
Review source links and research citations before sharing the pre-read with attendees.
Confirm owners, timeboxes, and required decisions with the meeting organizer before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not publish a generated agenda without checking sensitive Notion content and permissions.
Do not treat Codex research as final without verifying cited sources.
Do not use this skill for live transcription or attendance tracking.

faq.question: Does this skill require Notion access?
faq.answer: Yes. It depends on the Notion MCP connection and user-approved OAuth access.

faq.question: Can it create new Notion pages?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes creating agenda or pre-read pages when the Notion integration allows it.

faq.question: Can it update existing meeting pages?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes updating Notion pages when meeting details or plans change.

faq.question: Does it choose the meeting format?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides selection among status, decision, planning, retrospective, one-on-one, and brainstorming formats.

faq.question: Does it perform external research?
faq.answer: It can add concise Codex research with source links when outside context is useful.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential meetings?
faq.answer: Use restricted Notion permissions and review generated pages before sharing confidential information.
